title: Mouse Tissue Fixation with Paraformaldehyde for Fluorescent Reporter Mice
author: Paola Marcovecchio
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.vnge5bw

steps.title: Prepare 1
steps.instruction: Prepare 1.6% PFA in PBS (at least 20 mL per mouse, half to be used during perfusion, half to be used as storage buffer overnight) MAKE THIS FRESH, DO NOT USE ANYTHING OLDER THAN 24 HOURS. Also, make sure PFA has been stored away from light, this will degrade it.

steps.title: Prepare PBS with 2mM EDTA (at...
steps.instruction: Prepare PBS with 2mM EDTA (at least 10 mL per mouse).

steps.title: Sacrifice the mouse with CO 2
steps.instruction: Sacrifice the mouse with CO 2 . As soon as the mouse has stopped breathing, begin the procedure.

steps.title: Dissect open the thoracic cavity, taking...
steps.instruction: Dissect open the thoracic cavity, taking care not to puncture the heart with the scissors or forceps.

steps.title: Fill a 10mL syringe with 10...
steps.instruction: Fill a 10mL syringe with 10 mL, PBS with 2mM EDTA , attach needle to syringe.

steps.title: Insert needle into mouse’s right ventricle...
steps.instruction: Insert needle into mouse’s right ventricle (left side of heart when looking at the dissected mouse from your perspective). Slowly perfuse mouse until lungs turn white and liver has turned brown (instead of red).

steps.title: Fill the syringe with 10 mL, 1
steps.instruction: Fill the syringe with 10 mL, 1.6% PFA in PBS . Repeat perfusion. Mouse should appear stiff at the end of this perfusion.

steps.title: Remove tissue of interest carefully so...
steps.instruction: Remove tissue of interest carefully so that marks are not made in the tissue.

steps.title: Place tissue in 10 mL, remaining 1
steps.instruction: Place tissue in 10 mL, remaining 1.6% PFA in PBS in either 15 or 50mL conical (depending on how large the tissue is).

steps.title: For larger, thicker tissues, leave at...
steps.instruction: For larger, thicker tissues, leave at room temperature overnight ( 16:00:00 - 20:00:00 ) in a dark place or covered well in foil. For smaller tissues (i.e. lymph nodes) or for tissues that require antibody staining, fix at room temperature for a few hours (2-8, depending on downstream application).

steps.title: Wash with PBS 1x the next day, 3 times
steps.instruction: Wash with PBS 1x the next day, 3 times. Use at least 10 mL (or more) to wash the tube with the fixed tissue in it. Let tissue sit in fresh PBS wash about 00:20:00 each time in Place in 4 °C until ready to use.
